title: BBC精选
desc: 英国广播新闻，含中英字幕、讲解及听写训练。
transcript:
Vietnam's new president To Lam has held talks with his Chinese counterpart during a visit to Beijing.
 Chinese state media said President Xi Jinping expressed his willingness to work with To Lam to continuously strengthen their respective socialist causes.
 The 2 countries have sought to deepen already close economic ties amid the global trade upheaval from President Trump's tariffs.
 Legislators in the northeastern U.S. state of Maine have passed a bill that could make it the first state to put a moratorium on new data centers linked to the massive expansion of artificial intelligence. 
 The bill still needs approval from the governor. 
 A BBC investigation in Britain has revealed a shadow industry of legal advisers charging thousands of dollars to help migrants pretend to be gay in order to claim asylum and stay in the country.
 International students and migrant workers whose visas are due to run out are being given fake cover stories for their asylum claims. 
 Our Politics Investigation correspondent Billy Kenber reports. 
 Advisers to asvlum seekers are extracting fees from international students and workers who want to stay in the country. 
 One advisor told a BBC Undercover reporter his only hope was to claim asylum by pretending to be gay and sent him to an event for gay asylum seekers. 
 The BBC filmed men there saying they weren't genuinely gay. The adviser denied all wrongdoing. 
 The Home Office said anyone found trying to exploit the system will face the full force of the law. 
 Kanye West has postponed a concert in the French city of Marseille, scheduled for June. 
 It follows reports that the country's interior minister was seeking to block the performance because of the American rapper's historic anti-Semitic remarks. 
 Last week, the Wireless Festival in the UK was cancelled after West,  known as Ye, was denied entry to Britain. 
 West has apologized for his behavior, which he blamed on his bipolar disorder. And that's the latest world news from the BBC.

title: VOA慢速英语
desc: VOA官方授权，每日同步，适合初学者。
transcript: Scientists say they have found evidence of water on Mars. This discovery could mean that life once existed on the red planet. The research was published in the journal Nature.

title: VOA常速英语
desc: VOA官方授权，常速新闻（暂停更新说明已标注）。
transcript: The White House has ordered a significant budget cut, leading to the suspension of most VOA employees. The future of international broadcasting remains uncertain.

title: 经济学人合集
desc: 《经济学人》国际新闻周刊，政商精英必读。
transcript: Global inflation continues to challenge central banks. The Federal Reserve signaled further interest rate hikes. Economists are divided on whether a soft landing is possible.

title: VOA慢速-时事新闻
desc: VOA慢速 In the News 系列。
transcript: Health officials are concerned about a new virus variant spreading in several countries. Vaccination remains the best defense, according to experts.

title: 科学美国人
desc: 60秒科学，托福雅思听力绝佳材料。
transcript: A new study reveals that dolphins use unique whistles to identify each other. This suggests a complex social structure similar to human names.

title: NPR News
desc: 美国国家公共电台新闻，每月更新。
transcript: The Supreme Court heard arguments in a case that could reshape internet speech laws. Tech companies are watching closely.

title: TED演讲视频
desc: TED官方授权演讲，覆盖科技/设计/娱乐。
transcript: Creativity is not a talent, it's a way of operating. We need to create environments where people feel safe to fail and try new things.

title: AP News
desc: 美联社一分钟新闻，每日更新。
transcript: The latest jobs report shows stronger than expected growth. Unemployment remains at historic lows. Markets reacted positively to the news.
